channel.title: Particle-in-Cell Simulation on Ying-Shan Chen@TW ~ $
channel.description: Recent content in Particle-in-Cell Simulation on Ying-Shan Chen@TW ~ $

item.title: PIC-FLIP Fluids Simulation
item.description:
🔗 See more details about fluid simulation, forked from the projects in physical-based animation course at University of Toronto.

🔗 If you need my source code, please email me.

Waterfall

Maze

Lots of Fluid

Reference

  1. Physics-based animation lecture 10: Fluid Simulation

item.title: Full Three-dimensional Particle-In-Cell Simulation for Phase-matched Ion-based High-order Harmonic Generation in Water-window X-ray
item.description: “Full Three-dimensional Particle-In-Cell Simulation for Phase-matched Ion-based High-order Harmonic Generation in Water-window X-ray”, Chinese Journal of Physics, 2026 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cjph.2025.12.033

item.title: Two Stream Instability Simulation in Plasma Physics
item.description:
In this project, I used the Particle-in-Cell (PIC) simulation method to study the two-stream instability which is a fundamental plasma instability that arises when two particle populations move at different velocities. The system which is shown here is triggered by setting up a current in a plasma such that electrons and ions drift at different speeds.

As a result of these relative motions, energy is transferred into the plasma, leading to the excitation of plasma waves. These waves grow over time and play a key role in phenomena such as wave–particle interactions, turbulence, and anomalous transport in plasmas.

item.title: Particle-in-Cell Simulation of Phase-Matched High Harmonics Generation in Highly Ionized Plasmas
item.description:
Related Publication

  1. Numerical experiment of phase-matched ion-based high-order harmonic generation in the water-window x-ray spectral region via electromagnetic envelope equation, Optics Express, 2024
  2. Full Three-dimensional Particle-In-Cell Simulation for Phase-matched Ion-based High-order Harmonic Generation in Water-window X-ray, Chinese Journal of Physics, 2026
